text: Last F (2026). spade: Package (One Line, Title Case). R package version 0.0.0.9000.
code: @Manual{,   title = {spade: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)},   author = {First Last},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000}, }

what: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: Sheffield Probabilistic Artefact Date Elicitation (SPADE) package contains shiny app can used elicit probabilistic date estimates artefacts, based expert knowledge. developed part Quantifying Uncertainty Expert Archaeological Evidence (QUEADE) project funded Leverhulme Trust accompanies publication: Krzyzanska, M., May, K., Oakley, J. E., Wigg-Wolf, D., Buck, C. E., Quantifying uncertainty Expert Archaeological Dating Evidence: formal knowledge elicitation approach. Submitted Journal Archaeological Science.

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: Install devtools package already : Install latest version spade directly GitHub: Launch application:

what: Fit distributions to elicited probabilities — fitdist
title: Fit distributions to elicited probabilities — fitdist
text: Takes elicited probabilities inputs, fits parametric distributions using least squares cumulative distribution function. separate judgements multiple experts specified, function fit one set distributions per expert.

title: Fit distributions to elicited probabilities — fitdist
code: fitdist(   vals,   probs,   lower = -Inf,   upper = Inf,   weights = 1,   tdf = 3,   expertnames = NULL,   excludelogt = FALSE )

title: Fit distributions to elicited probabilities — fitdist
text: vals vector elicited values one expert, matrix elicited values multiple experts (one column per expert). Note elicited judgement X form P(X<= valsi,j) = probsi,j probs vector elicited probabilies one expert, matrix elicited values multiple experts (one column per expert). single vector can used probabilities expert. expert, least one non-zero probability less 0.4, least one elicited probability less 1 greater 0.6. Exponential distributions can fitted specifying one limit (lower upper) one probability 0 1. lower single lower limit uncertain quantity X, vector different lower limits expert. Specifying lower limit allow fitting distributions bounded . upper single upper limit uncertain quantity X, vector different lower limits expert. Specifying lower limit upper limit allow fitting Beta distribution. weights vector matrix weights corresponding vals weighted least squares used parameter fitting. tdf number degrees freedom used fitting t-distribution. expertnames Vector names use expert. excludelogt Set TRUE exclude log-t mirror log-t identifying best fitting distribution.

title: Fit distributions to elicited probabilities — fitdist
text: object class elicitation. list containing elements Normal Parameters fitted normal distributions. Student.t Parameters fitted t distributions. Note (X - location) / scale standard t distribution. degrees freedom fitted; specified argument fitdist. Skewnormal Parameters fitted skew-normal distribution. skew-normal distribution implemented using sn package. See sn::dsn details. distribution requires least three elicited probabilities, including least one interval (0, 0.4) (0.6, 1). Gamma Parameters fitted gamma distributions. Note E(X - lower) = shape / rate. Log.normal Parameters fitted log normal distributions: mean standard deviation log (X - lower). Log.Student.t Parameters fitted log student t distributions. Note (log(X- lower) - location) / scale standard t distribution. degrees freedom fitted; specified argument fitdist. Beta Parameters fitted beta distributions. X scaled interval 0,1 via Y = (X - lower)/(upper - lower), E(Y) = shape1 / (shape1 + shape2). mirrorgamma Parameters ('mirror') gamma distributions fitted Y = upper - X. Note E(Y) = shape / rate. mirrorlognormal Parameters ('mirror') log normal distributions fitted Y = upper - X. mirrorlogt Parameters ('mirror') log Student-t distributions fitted Y = upper - X. Note (log(Y) - location) / scale standard t distribution. degrees freedom fitted; specified argument fitdist. ssq Sum squared errors fitted distribution expert. error difference elicited cumulative probability corresponding fitted cumulative probability. best.fitting best fitting distribution expert, determined smallest sum squared errors. Note three judgements , likely skew-normal, three parameter distribution. vals elicited values used fit distributions. probs elicited probabilities used fit distributions. limits lower upper limits specified expert (+/- Inf specified).

title: Fit distributions to elicited probabilities — fitdist
text: least squares parameter values found numerically using optim command. Starting values distribution parameters chosen based simple normal approximation: linear interpolation used estimate 0.4, 0.5 0.6 quantiles, starting parameter values chosen setting E(X) equal 0.5th quantile, Var(X) = (0.6 quantile - 0.4 quantile)^2 / 0.25. Note arguments lower upper included elicited values cumulative distribution function. include judgement P(X<=)=0, values 0 must included vals probs respectively.

title: Fit distributions to elicited probabilities — fitdist
Fit distributions to elicited probabilities — fitdist
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # One expert, with elicited probabilities # P(X<20)=0.25, P(X<30)=0.5, P(X<50)=0.75 # and X>0. v <- c(20,30,50) p <- c(0.25,0.5,0.75) fitdist(vals=v, probs=p, lower=0)  # Now add a second expert, with elicited probabilities # P(X<55)=0.25, P(X<60=0.5), P(X<70)=0.75 v <- matrix(c(20,30,50,55,60,70),3,2) p <- c(0.25,0.5,0.75) fitdist(vals=v, probs=p, lower=0)  # Two experts, different elicited quantiles and limits. # Expert A: P(X<50)=0.25, P(X<60=0.5), P(X<65)=0.75, and provides bounds 10<X<100 # Expert B: P(X<40)=0.33, P(X<50=0.5), P(X<60)=0.66, and provides bounds 0<X v <- matrix(c(50,60,65,40,50,60),3,2) p <- matrix(c(.25,.5,.75,.33,.5,.66),3,2) l <- c(10,0) u <- c(100, Inf) fitdist(vals=v, probs=p, lower=l, upper=u) } # }

what: Plot the elicited cumulative probabilities — makeCDFPlot
title: Plot the elicited cumulative probabilities — makeCDFPlot
text: Plots elicited cumulative probabilities , optionally, fitted CDF. Elicited shown filled circles, limits shown clear circles.

title: Plot the elicited cumulative probabilities — makeCDFPlot
code: makeCDFPlot(   lower,   v,   p,   upper,   fontsize = 12,   fit = NULL,   dist = NULL,   showFittedCDF = FALSE,   showQuantiles = FALSE,   ql = 0.05,   qu = 0.95,   ex = 1,   sf = 3,   xaxisLower = lower,   xaxisUpper = upper,   xlab = "x",   ylab = expression(P(X <= x)),   min_val = NULL,   max_val = NULL )

title: Plot the elicited cumulative probabilities — makeCDFPlot
text: lower lower limit uncertain quantity v vector values, value x Pr(X<=x) = p set elicited probabilities p vector probabilities, value p Pr(X<=x) = p set elicited probabilities upper upper limit uncertain quantity fontsize font size used plot fit object class elicitation dist fitted distribution plotted. Options "normal", "t", "skewnormal", "gamma", "lognormal", "logt","beta", "mirrorgamma", "mirrorlognormal", "mirrorlogt" "hist" (histogram fit) showFittedCDF logical. fitted distribution function displayed? showQuantiles logical. quantiles fitted distribution function displayed? ql lower quantile displayed. qu upper quantile displayed. ex object fit contains judgements multiple experts, (single) expert's judgements show. sf number significant figures displayed. xaxisLower lower limit x-axis. xaxisUpper upper limit x-axis. xlab x-axis label. ylab y-axis label.

title: Plot the elicited cumulative probabilities — makeCDFPlot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ vQuartiles <- c(30, 35, 45) pQuartiles<- c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75) myfit <- fitdist(vals = vQuartiles, probs = pQuartiles, lower = 0) makeCDFPlot(lower = 0, v = vQuartiles, p = pQuartiles,  upper = 100, fit = myfit, dist = "gamma",  showFittedCDF = TRUE, showQuantiles = TRUE)   } # }

what: Plot the fitted density function for one or more experts — plotfit
title: Plot the fitted density function for one or more experts — plotfit
text: Plots fitted density function one experts. Can also plot fitted linear pool one expert. plotting density function one expert, linear pool , can also indicated desired lower upper fitted quantiles.

title: Plot the fitted density function for one or more experts — plotfit
code: plotfit(   fit,   d = "best",   xl = -Inf,   xu = Inf,   yl = 0,   yu = NA,   ql = NA,   qu = NA,   lp = FALSE,   ex = NA,   sf = 3,   ind = TRUE,   lpw = 1,   fs = 12,   lwd = 1,   xlab = "x",   ylab = expression(f[X](x)),   legend_full = TRUE,   percentages = FALSE,   returnPlot = FALSE,   showPlot = TRUE,   startDate = NA,   endDate = NA,   nBins = NA,   ybreaks = NA,   chips = NA )

title: Plot the fitted density function for one or more experts — plotfit
text: fit object class elicitation. d distribution fitted expert's probabilities. Options "normal", "t", "skewnormal", "gamma", "lognormal", "logt","beta", "mirrorgamma", "mirrorlognormal", "mirrorlogt" "hist" (histogram fit), "best" (best fitting) xl lower limit x-axis. default 0.001 quantile fitted distribution (0.001 quantile fitted normal distribution, histogram fit chosen). xu upper limit x-axis. default 0.999 quantile fitted distribution (0.999 quantile fitted normal distribution, histogram fit chosen). yl lower limit y-axis. Default value 0. yu upper limit y-axis. set automatically specified. ql lower quantile indicated density function plot. displayed plotting density function single expert. qu upper quantile indicated density function plot. displayed plotting density function single expert. lp multiple experts, set lp = TRUE plot linear pool. ex judgements elicited multiple experts, density plot one expert required, expert used plot. sf number significant figures displayed parameter values. ind plotting linear pool, set ind = FALSE suppress plotting individual density functions. lpw vector weights used linear pool, unequal weighting desired. fs font size used plot. lwd line width used plot. xlab string expression giving x-axis label. ylab string expression giving y-axis label. legend_full plotting linear pool, set ind = TRUE expert plotted different colour, ind = FALSE expert plotted colour, reducing legend size. percentages Set TRUE use percentages x-axis. returnPlot Set TRUE return plot ggplot object. showPlot Set FALSE suppress displaying plot.

title: Plot the fitted density function for one or more experts — plotfit
Plot the fitted density function for one or more experts — plotfit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Two experts # Expert 1 states P(X<30)=0.25, P(X<40)=0.5, P(X<50)=0.75 # Expert 2 states P(X<20)=0.25, P(X<25)=0.5, P(X<35)=0.75 # Both experts state 0<X<100.  v <- matrix(c(30, 40, 50, 20, 25, 35), 3, 2) p <- c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75) myfit <- fitdist(vals = v, probs = p, lower = 0, upper = 100)  # Plot both fitted densities, using the best fitted distribution plotfit(myfit)  # Plot a fitted beta distribution for expert 2, and show 5th and 95th percentiles plotfit(myfit, d = "beta", ql = 0.05, qu = 0.95, ex = 2)   # Plot a linear pool, giving double weight to expert 1 plotfit(myfit,  lp = T, lpw = c(2,1))   # Plot a linear pool, giving double weight to expert 1, # show 5th and 95th percentiles, surpress plotting of individual distributions, # and force use of Beta distributions plotfit(myfit, d = "beta",  lp = T, lpw = c(2,1), ql = 0.05, qu = 0.95, ind=FALSE ) } # }

title: Run the Shiny app — run_spade
Run the Shiny app — run_spade
